core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity

## 1. Essence
N-Infinity remains the governed swarm layer of N1Hub, but it now routes its live contract through four bounded sub-hubs so runtime substrate, worker roster, upstream signal sources, and governance metrics stay explicit instead of collapsing into one dense role-and-physics directory.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `runtime-fabric` owns the execution substrate: background runtime, Symphony lane, job envelopes, continuity state, and night-window posture.
- `swarm-roster` owns the named N-Infinity worker family: Weaver, Gardener, Parliament, Innovator, Reminder, Risk Detector, and Suggestion Agent.
- `signal-fabric` owns the external lanes and operator-facing surfaces that feed context, urgency, and supply into the swarm.
- `governance-metrics` owns the relation semantics, confidence geometry, and epistemic memory the swarm uses to reason about graph quality.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent hub now keeps only the top-level relationship to CapsuleOS, N1Hub, To Dig Deep, the broader Mining Company frame, and the enclosing N-Infinity project. Detailed runtime atoms and worker topology flow through the four sub-hubs so N-Infinity remains a navigable swarm contract rather than a gravity well of mixed substrate, roles, and reasoning law.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity is now the sovereign parent hub for four bounded swarm surfaces: runtime fabric, swarm roster, signal fabric, and governance metrics. The parent keeps only top-level doctrinal and project relationships and routes detailed swarm atoms through sub-hubs so the live contract stays legible and reviewable. This matters because the parent should express the canonical operating shape of the swarm while leaving substrate, worker, signal, and reasoning details to bounded surfaces that can evolve without collapsing into one dense directory.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Refactored parent hub to route through 4 sub-hubs. Outbound links reduced from 26 to ≤9.
